chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1143 of 2022 Date Order with Signature of Hon’ble Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on Office objection at Flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of main case 21.12.2022 Mr. Muhammad Nasir Malik, Advocate for the petitioner Mr. Ihsan Ali Lund Baloch, Advocate for Respondent No.6 Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant Advocate General <><><><>..<><><><> Mr. Shahid Hussain Jogi, Advocate, files power as well as para wise comments on behalf of respondent No.5, which are taken on record, copy of para wise comments is supplied to learned counsel for the petitioner. The petitioner, who was initially inducted as Chowkidar (BPS-01) in Local Government Department, Government of Sindh and afterwards promoted and retired as Octroi Chowkidar (BPS-02) on 30.07.2000 on attaining the age of superannuation of 60 years. Through instant petition, the petitioner seeks directions to the respondents to pay the differential pension amount of Rs.6,23,560.55 from August 2015 to April 2022 as per statement / annexure ‘C’ to ‘C-VI’. He also seeks directions to respondents to pay complete pension amount from the month of May, 2022 onwards as well as Gratuity amounting to Rs.113000/- as per rules. Para wise comments have been filed by respondent No.5, which reflect that the service record of the petitioner is lying with respondent No.6 (Town Officer, Town Committee, Setharja), the same shall be got audited from the Audit Department and thereafter the grievance of the petitioner would be redressed. Let respondent No.6 hand over the service record of the petitioner to respondent No.5 within fifteen days hereof, without fail and

Para wise comments have been filed by respondent No.5, which reflect that the service record of the petitioner is lying with respondent No.6 (Town Officer, Town Committee, Setharja), the same shall be got audited from the Audit Department and thereafter the grievance of the petitioner would be redressed. Let respondent No.6 hand over the service record of the petitioner to respondent No.5 within fifteen days hereof, without fail and Page 2 of 2 thereafter respondent No.5 shall proceed with the case of the petitioner for payment of the aforementioned differential amount as well as the Gratuity amounting to Rs.113000/-. So far the grievance of the petitioner with regard to payment of monthly pension is concerned, the same shall be adjusted after the aforementioned differential pension amount by the respondent No.5. This exercise shall be done by respondent No.5 expeditiously and preferably within sixty days hereof. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. Judge Judge ARBROHI
